title: Global Mobility Lawyer's Global Mobility Summit | Fragomen, Del Rey, Bernsen & Loewy LLP
content:
## Global Mobility Lawyer’s Global Mobility Summit
June 10, 2026 | 8:30 AM — 6:00 PM BST
Fragomen in London, United Kingdom
T: +44 (0) 20 7090 9235
Fragomen in London, United Kingdom
T: +44 (0) 20 7090 9235
Fragomen in London, United Kingdom
T: +44 (0) 20 7090 9235
The Minster Building, 21 Mincing Lane
Fragomen is pleased to be participating in the Global Mobility Summit – London , hosted by Global Mobility Lawyer, on June 10, 2026 in London.
This full-day conference will bring together senior in-house counsel, global mobility managers, HR professionals, and private practice lawyers to explore the latest trends and developments shaping cross-border workforce management. Discussions will focus on key areas including employment, immigration, social security, and tax, providing a strategic perspective on the evolving regulatory landscape.
Fragomen will contribute to the program in the session What digital immigration systems mean for global mobility . Governments worldwide are rapidly transforming immigration processes, shifting from traditional paper-based systems to digital solutions such as e-visas, biometric identity tools, online right-to-work checks, and enhanced data-sharing between authorities. This panel will examine the implications of these changes for multinational employers, including compliance challenges and the increasing importance of immigration as a board-level strategic risk.
Joining the panel is Fragomen’s Kelly Chua , Practice Leader , who will share insights on how organizations can adapt their mobility programs in response to the digitization of immigration systems and evolving compliance expectations.
This conference is designed for senior professionals involved in global workforce strategy and compliance, including legal, HR, and mobility leaders who are navigating complex international regulatory environments.
Click the register button above to join this event or for more information get in touch with us at events@fragomen.com .

## Explore more at Fragomen
Fragomen earned top national and regional rankings in Chambers USA 2026, including its 13th consecutive year in Band 1 nationwide.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold examines the remaining opportunities under Ireland’s winding-down Immigrant Investor Programme and what they mean for eligible investors and their families.
Government Affairs Strategy Director Shuyeb Muquit analyses the UK’s latest net migration decline, exploring what the data reveals about policy impact, economic trade‑offs and the risks of over‑correction in future migration strategy.
Partner Bo Cooper discusses the practical considerations facing employers and foreign nationals amid recent changes to green card processing.
In this #FragomenFC episode, Partner Rick Lamanna, Senior Manager Sergio Flores and Senior Associate Jake Paul Minster discuss final travel and immigration considerations ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold discusses Ireland’s citizenship framework and the gaps that can remain following reforms to birthright citizenship.
In this video, Partner Diana Quintas discusses how Brazil’s family reunion visa supports long-term international assignments and outlines key eligibility, documentation and compliance considerations for employers and families relocating to Brazil.
Attorney María José Clarke explores Chile’s intensified immigration enforcement framework under President José Antonio Kast, examining new employer compliance obligations, work authorization rules for foreign nationals and the legal and financial consequences of unauthorized employment.
Senior Manager Jonathan Hill examines how the UK’s new RAG rating system and stricter compliance metrics could shape universities’ approaches to international student recruitment and sponsorship compliance.
Knowledge Management Director Ana Sofia Walsh and Senior Client Engagement Manager Soraya Driessen examine the European Commission’s EU Inc. proposal and its potential to reshape corporate structures across the EU, highlighting the resulting complexities and unresolved questions for immigration, work authorization and cross-border workforce mobility.
